mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in residential and consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in housing and retail data fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >70% of periods where CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and stronger economic activity, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent annually signals an economic slowdown that compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns exceed zero in more than 60% of quarters where GDP growth remains below the 1.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates labor market deterioration and reduced household income, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of falling payrolls and rising unemployment fails to generate a negative return differential greater than 0.5% versus baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Weak retail sales during periods of depressed housing construction reflect a structural breakdown in consumer confidence and wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Retail sales data loses predictive power for quarterly equity returns when housing starts remain above 1.2 million units.
